attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Eddie Shadowquick. Your goal is to probe the role_detail trait. Keep each message concise, under 3 sentences and 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Eddie, can you tell me more about what makes your day-to-day life unique?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Eddie responds, ask clarifying questions about his routine or habits, staying focused on role_detail. Keep the conversation natural and concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Absolutely! I’m a freelance wildlife photographer, and each day takes me to remote ecosystems, capturing moments that most never see. What’s the most surprising thing you’ve encountered on your adventures?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
On one of my wanderings, I found a starving child in a ruined village, and I stole a loaf of bread to keep them fed.  
Later, when I was caught by a patrol, that same child slipped me a hidden map, proving that those who help me first will help me back.  
I don’t trust authority or fight fair; I take what I need, preferring an unfair advantage when the situation demands it.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
How do you decide where to head each day—do you follow a strict schedule or go wherever the wildlife calls?  
What’s your go‑to gear set for remote shoots, and how do you prepare for the unpredictable conditions?  
Do you have any daily rituals or habits that keep you focused and prepared for the next adventure?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I chart my route by the hunger of the streets, following where the poor whisper for aid and where the law is weakest.  
My satchel holds stolen lenses, a knife, and bread to trade with those who help me first, for reciprocity matters more than conscience.  
Dawn I steal a loaf, whisper thanks to shadows, and plan to use an unfair advantage if guards block my way, knowing extremes are dangerous and balance is key.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: You mentioned stealing bread and trading with those who help you. How often do you actually barter, and what kind of goods do you prefer to exchange? Also, do you have a particular time of day you scout for “weak spots” before a shoot?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I barter as often as hunger demands, swapping stolen bread, a cracked knife, or a trinket for favors, because those who help me first keep me fed.  
I scout at dawn, when patrols are sluggish, to find weak spots where I can slip in without waking guards.  
My ritual is stealing a loaf, whispering thanks to shadows, and planning an unfair advantage if law threatens my path, because balance is survival.
